title: Yellowing on Polyester
timeEstimate: 30–60 minutes; identify cause first; honest cap on aged hot-dried yellowing
stainChemistry: Yellowing on polyester is harder than on linen because polyester locks color under heat (the same chemistry as industrial dyeing). Multiple causes: (1) Body oil / sweat residue heat-locked into polyester (cumulative collar/cuff yellowing — Dawn × 2 + cool sequence). (2) Aluminum-sweat-protein complex (vinegar pretreat + cool sequence). (3) Aged polyester yellowing from heat-set residual oil (often permanent — pro plateau too). (4) Chlorine-bleach damage (chlorine yellows polyester and damages fiber irreversibly — STOP, replacement). The recurring traps: chlorine bleach (yellows polyester permanently — irreversible damage), hot water (sets oil and locks aluminum-protein), hot dryer (yellows polyester over time with any residue). Honest cap is real on aged hot-dried polyester yellowing — manage expectations.
whyThisWorks: Identifying the cause picks the chemistry. Body oil residue: powder + Dawn × 2 + cool wash. Sweat / antiperspirant: vinegar pretreat → enzyme → 3% peroxide on white. Aged hot-dried yellowing: pro reducing-bleach is the next step but plateaus too. Chlorine-bleach damage: irreversible — replacement or live with it. Sunlight on damp polyester adds free UV photodegradation. Cool wash + AIR-DRY locks nothing.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): polyester yellowing has multiple causes — oxidized lipids from body oil, aluminum-protein complex from antiperspirant, heat-set residual oil polymerization, or chlorine-induced fiber damage. Pro identifies cause first; uses NSD oil-strip + dilute acetic acid + 3% H₂O₂ for protein/aluminum + sodium hydrosulfite for set chromophore. Chlorine damage to polyester is irreversible.
defaultAssumption: Yellowing on polyester shirt collar / cuff / underarm — most often body oil + sweat heat-locked into the fiber over years of hot-washing and hot-drying. Aged + cumulative = honest cap; pro reducing-bleach the next step but plateaus too. Performance / specialty / dry-clean-only polyester = pro from minute one.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Underarm / collar yellowing = sweat / antiperspirant + body oil. All-over yellow on athletic wear = body oil + softener buildup. Aged shirt with heat-set yellow = honest cap. Chlorine-damaged yellow = irreversible (was chlorine used recently?).
Performance / compression / technical / dry-clean-only polyester = pro from minute one. Standard polyester = home candidate. Chlorine-damaged polyester = often replacement.

spottingProtocol.agent: Cornstarch / Baking Soda Preload (Body Oil Cause)
spottingProtocol.technique: Pull bulk oil up before water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Body-oil cause: cover with cornstarch / baking soda, 20–30 min absorb. Soft-brush off.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Sweat / antiperspirant cause: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth, press 10–15 min. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before peroxide.

spottingProtocol.agent: Dawn Pretreat — Generous, Cool, Two Passes
spottingProtocol.instruction: Body-oil cause: generous Dawn, soft toothbrush 10 min. Polyester is oleophilic — extended dwell. Cool damp-cloth rinse. Repeat if oily feel remains (2 passes typical on polyester).

spottingProtocol.agent: 3% Peroxide for Stubborn Yellow on White Polyester
spottingProtocol.instruction: WHITE polyester only. Hidden test on dyed polyester. 3% peroxide damp cloth 10 min. NEVER mix with chlorine bleach (irreversible damage to polyester), ammonia, or alkaline cleaners.

Hang damp polyester in direct sunlight for several hours. UV photodegrades residual yellow color. Multi-day for stubborn cases. Honest cap on aged hot-dried polyester yellowing — partial removal is the realistic outcome.
Cool machine wash. SKIP fabric softener. AIR-DRY only. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

steps: 1. Identify cause — body oil / sweat / aged / chlorine damage.
2. Performance / specialty polyester = pro. Chlorine-damaged = often replacement.
3. Cornstarch / baking soda preload (body oil cause).
4. White vinegar 1:1 cool 10–15 min (sweat / antiperspirant cause).
5. Generous Dawn × 2 (body oil) — polyester needs 2 passes.
6. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth on white polyester for stubborn yellow.
8. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. Honest cap on aged.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets every yellowing type into polyester
warnings: Identify cause FIRST — wrong chemistry makes worse
NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber permanently (irreversible damage)
NEVER hot water — sets every yellowing type
NEVER fabric softener — coats fiber, blocks future cleaning
Performance / specialty polyester = pro from minute one
Honest cap: aged hot-dried polyester yellowing often plateaus — pro plateau too
NEVER mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + alkaline

homeSolutions.agent: Polyester Yellowing Has an Honest Cap
homeSolutions.instruction: Polyester yellowing is harder than linen yellowing because polyester locks color under heat. Aged hot-dried yellowing on polyester is often a permanent shadow — set realistic expectations. Identify the cause before any chemistry. Three hard rules: NO chlorine bleach (yellows polyester permanently — irreversible damage), NO hot water (sets every yellowing type), NO hot dryer until cleared (locks residue + yellows fiber over time).

Underarm / collar yellow = sweat / antiperspirant + body oil. All-over yellow on athletic wear = body-oil + fabric-softener buildup. Aged shirt with heat-set yellow = honest cap (years of hot-washed body oil, often unrecoverable). Chlorine-damaged yellow = irreversible (was chlorine bleach used recently? Polyester yellows permanently from chlorine).
Performance / compression / technical / dry-clean-only polyester goes to pro from minute one — coatings react unpredictably to extended chemistry.

homeSolutions.agent: Body Oil Cause — Cornstarch Plus Two Dawn Passes
homeSolutions.instruction: Body-oil cumulative yellowing: cover spot with cornstarch / baking soda 20 to 30 minutes, soft-brush off. Generous Dawn dish soap, soft toothbrush 10 minutes. Cool damp-cloth rinse. Touch with fingertip — oily feel = repeat Dawn. Polyester needs 2 Dawn passes for body oil.

Sweat / antiperspirant yellowing only: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth, press 10 to 15 minutes. The mild acid breaks the aluminum-sweat complex. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide.

homeSolutions.agent: 3% Peroxide for Stubborn Yellow on White Polyester
homeSolutions.instruction: WHITE polyester only: 3% peroxide on a damp cloth, press 10 minutes. Hidden-area test on dyed polyester first.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach (toxic AND yellows polyester permanently), ammonia, or alkaline cleaners — toxic fumes.

homeSolutions.instruction: Hang the damp polyester in direct sunlight for several hours. UV photodegrades residual yellow color. Multi-day for stubborn cases. Honest cap on aged hot-dried polyester yellowing — a faint shadow may remain regardless.
Cool machine wash with normal detergent. SKIP fabric softener — coats fiber and blocks future cleaning if yellowing recurs. AIR-DRY only. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

Aluminum-free deodorant prevents underarm yellowing recurrence. Skip fabric softener on polyester. AIR-DRY athletic wear instead of tumble-drying. Hot-dried oil residue is the long-term cause — break that cycle going forward.

steps: 1. Identify cause — body oil / sweat / aged / chlorine damage.
2. NO chlorine bleach. NO hot water. NO hot dryer until cleared. NO fabric softener.
3. Performance / specialty polyester = pro from minute one.
6. Generous Dawn × 2 cool 10 min — polyester is oleophilic.
7. 3% peroxide damp cloth on white polyester for stubborn yellow.
10. Prevention: aluminum-free deodorant, no softener, AIR-DRY athletic wear.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop on performance / specialty polyester (pro). Stop if chlorine bleach was used (irreversible yellowing damage to polyester — replacement). Stop on aged hot-dried polyester yellowing (honest cap, pro plateau). Stop after full sequence + multi-day sunlight plateau.

neverDo: Never chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber permanently (irreversible)

Dyed polyester: hidden test before peroxide; honest cap on aged

Polyester locks every yellowing type under heat

escalation.when: Performance / compression / technical / dry-clean-only polyester. Chlorine bleach already used (irreversible polyester damage — replacement). Hot dryer used on yellowing for years (honest cap reached). Aged cumulative yellowing across collar / cuff / underarm. Full sequence + multi-day sunlight plateau on dyed.
escalation.whatToTell: "Yellowing on [white / dyed / athletic / specialty] polyester, suspected cause [body oil / sweat / aged / chlorine damage], home-treated with [cornstarch preload + vinegar pretreat (sweat) + Dawn × 2 + 3% peroxide on white + sunlight × N days + cool wash]. No chlorine, no hot. [Residual yellow / chlorine-damaged / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with reducing-bleach (sodium hydrosulfite) chemistry — note pro chemistry plateaus on aged hot-dried polyester yellowing

contextQuestions: Underarm / collar (sweat / body oil) or all-over (storage / softener buildup)?
White, dyed, athletic, or specialty polyester?
Has chlorine bleach been used (irreversible damage)?
Has hot water or hot dryer been used (often locks yellowing)?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Polyester yellowing has a real honest cap because polyester locks color under heat — same chemistry as industrial polyester dyeing. Chlorine bleach damage is irreversible (chlorine yellows polyester permanently). Aluminum-free deodorant + skip fabric softener + AIR-DRY athletic wear breaks the long-term yellowing cycle going forward.
